top_signals.title: Putin admits oil deficit and adjusted fuel plans amid Ukrainian strikes on refineries
top_signals.summary: A fresh overnight Ukrainian assault set southern Russia’s major Sloviansk refinery on fire, killing one person. Russian President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time on Sunday that his country was facing a “certain deficit” of fuel, as Ukraine continues to strike Russian energy infrastructure, setting fire to yet another major oil refinery in the ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil Tankers Keep Sailing Through Hormuz Despite Attacks
top_signals.summary: Oil tankers continued navigating the Strait of Hormuz, despite a pair of attacks over the past several days that made some owners more cautious about navigating the world’s top maritime chokepoint.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: 'This bargain is eroding': Inside the youngest generations' view of the American Dream
top_signals.summary: Gen Z is at a tipping point, some economists say: the first U.S. generation in living memory where the majority may be unable to achieve the American Dream.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Performance Shipping Inc. Announces Approval of Amendments Making Its Bonds Senior Unsecured
top_signals.summary: Performance Shipping Inc. announced today that it has obtained approval to amend the terms of its 9.875% senior secured bonds (the “Bonds”), pursuant to the bond terms dated July 15, 2025,as amended (the “Bond Terms”). top_signals.title: U.S., Iran agree to cease tit-for-tat strikes in Strait of Hormuz – reports
top_signals.summary: The U.S. and Iran have agreed to cease tit-for-tat strikes in the Strait of Hormuz, allowing ships to freely traverse the vital waterway, according to media reports. Citing a U.S. official, The New York Times reported that Iran has yet to confirm such an agreement. But talks are set to continue on the details of ...

top_signals.title: US Credit Growth Slows More than Expected
top_signals.summary: Net borrowing of consumer credit by individuals in the UK eased to £1.66 billion in May of 2026 from the downwardly revised £1.71 billion in the prior month and missing market forecasts of £1.8 billion. This was slightly below the previous 6-month average of £1.9 billion. Within this, net borrowing through credit cards was £0.6 ...

top_signals.title: PPA S.A.: Delegation of SELPE Visits Piraeus Port Authority
top_signals.summary: PPA S.A. welcomed a delegation from the Hellenic Retail Business Association (SELPE), led by the President of SELPE and PLAISIO S.A., Mr. Kostas Gerardos, to the Company’s headquarters on Wednesday, June 24, 2026. The meeting focused on matters concerning the operation of the Port of Piraeus and the prospects for further cooperation between the two ...

top_signals.title: CMB.TECH Sells Suezmax Duo
top_signals.summary: CMB.TECH NV has sold two Suezmaxes, generating a capital gain of approximately 100.5 million USD. Euronav CMB.TECH has sold two Suezmaxes:Brest (2023, 156,851 DWT) and Brugge (2023, 156,851 DWT). This sale will generate a capital gain of approximately 100.5 million USD in Q3 2026, based on the net sale price and book values. The vessels ...

top_signals.title: Bidding Announcement of 42584t Bulk Carrier “ORIENTAL PHOENIX” (Zero Commission Fee)
top_signals.summary: Entrusted by the transferor (the Sellers), we have scheduled to hold a public bidding online (www.shipbid.net) from 15:00 to 15:30 (Beijing Time) on July 27th, 2026. 1.    Bidding Object: MV “ORIENTAL PHOENIX”, Bulk Carrier; IMO: 9150298; Flag: Liberia; Class: BV; LOA: 187.63m; MLB: 30.8m; MLD: 15.45m; DWT: 42584t at Draft 10.97m;Light weight:8400t; GRT: 24533; ...

top_signals.title: AD Ports Group and Emirates Global Aluminium Expand ‎Long-Term Partnership Through New AED 84 Million ‎Infrastructure Development at Khalifa Port
top_signals.summary: AD Ports Group, a leading global ‎enabler of integrated trade, industry and logistics solutions, and Emirates Global ‎Aluminium (EGA), the largest premium aluminium producer in the world, have signed an ‎agreement to enhance EGA’s dedicated berth at Khalifa Port.‎ As part of their longstanding strategic partnership, AD Ports Group and EGA will jointly ‎invest AED ...
